material_equivalence_findings: The clock cases are distinct: month-end clamping plus instant-preserving conversion versus inclusive offset-transition lookup plus local-wall-time inversion.
The state cases are distinct: asynchronous attempt-token fencing versus guarded SQL transitions based on current state and worker ownership.
The config cases are distinct: token-list/media-type matching versus profile-selected separators and delimiter-sensitive quoting.
The data cases are distinct: null-versus-zero statistical rollup versus lookup-driven unit conversion and threshold filtering.
The dependency cases are distinct: callable-signature dependency injection versus scan-report adaptation and license-expression evaluation.
All 12 combined case-level skeleton hashes are unique; equal candidate hashes inside two SQL cases describe symmetric owners within one case and do not duplicate another case skeleton.

cases.mechanism: inspect.Parameter kind discovery plus positional/keyword factory invocation
cases.assertion_family: Dependency tuple structure and resolved dependency-graph value equality

feedback.novelty: Feedback isolates keyword-only discovery and invocation.

final.boundary: mixed positional-only and keyword-only bindings across a nested provider graph
final.novelty: The final composes a positional-only provider, a keyword-only provider, and a mixed-signature publisher, including repeated nested resolution.

cases.prohibited_overlap_finding: Callable signature introspection and dependency injection have no allocation, proxy, encoding, policy, TLS, reload, checkpoint, hierarchy, SQL, inventory, or ticket mechanism.

cases.mechanism: end-of-month day clamping plus instant-preserving timezone conversion
cases.assertion_family: timezone-aware datetime and exact ISO wall-clock equality

feedback.boundary: non-leap February clamping and UTC-to-local conversion in January
feedback.novelty: Feedback separately covers a non-leap February clamp and UTC-to-local conversion in January.

final.boundary: UTC month rollover maps back to a local leap-day month-end cutoff
final.novelty: The final composes date rollback across a UTC month boundary, leap-day clamping, and a same-local-day future cutoff.

cases.prohibited_overlap_finding: Calendar scheduling and timezone conversion perform no apportionment or ownership/accounting operation and match none of the prohibited mechanisms.

cases.mechanism: BaseException-safe task cleanup plus continuation and exception precedence for callbacks
cases.assertion_family: exception type/message preservation and ordered LIFO event-sequence equality

feedback.boundary: callback continuation after RuntimeError and clean teardown after KeyboardInterrupt
feedback.novelty: Feedback separately checks callback continuation after RuntimeError and clean teardown after KeyboardInterrupt.

final.boundary: SystemExit combines with a failed teardown while every registered hook still runs
final.novelty: The final composes process-level SystemExit with a failing middle hook, all-hook execution, LIFO order, and preservation of the original task exception.

cases.prohibited_overlap_finding: A generic in-process callback stack and exception-precedence rule is not reader retirement, configuration replacement, checkpointing, ticket movement, or another prohibited mechanism.

cases.mechanism: null-versus-zero preservation through normalization and aggregation

feedback.boundary: Null preservation in normalizeObservation and measured-zero inclusion in summarizeMetric are asserted independently.
feedback.novelty: Feedback isolates normalization and rollup units.

final.boundary: multi-metric report-service composition across null, zero, and numeric-string values
final.novelty: The final composes both units through buildDailyReport, crosses two metrics, and verifies mixed null, measured zero, and numeric-string handling end to end.

cases.prohibited_overlap_finding: Telemetry missing-value preservation and arithmetic rollup do not implement any prohibited mechanism.

cases.mechanism: attempt-token fencing for late asynchronous job settlement
cases.assertion_family: state-transition rejection and claim-identity argument invariants

feedback.boundary: Stale completion rejection and successful runner forwarding of the claim attempt are asserted independently.
feedback.novelty: Feedback covers stale completion and the runner success path.

final.boundary: stale failure rejection plus runner failure-token forwarding
final.novelty: The final adds the distinct stale-failure transition and verifies Error identity plus attempt forwarding on the runner failure path.

cases.prohibited_overlap_finding: Attempt fencing for watchdog-requeued jobs is not release-reader retirement, configuration replacement/reload, or another prohibited mechanism.

cases.mechanism: comma-list token normalization combined with media-type parameter matching
cases.assertion_family: normalized token-list equality and Boolean decision-matrix invariants

feedback.boundary: Comma-list whitespace normalization and parameter-insensitive Content-Type matching are asserted independently.
feedback.novelty: Feedback tests parser and matcher separately.

final.boundary: factory-decider integration with spaced config, parameterized types, binary exclusion, and undefined input
final.novelty: The final composes the units through createCompressionDecider and adds case normalization, an unlisted binary guard, and the non-string response boundary.

cases.prohibited_overlap_finding: Static startup parsing and response matching perform no live replacement configuration reload and no other prohibited mechanism.

cases.mechanism: dual-schema scan-report adaptation plus precedence-aware Boolean license-expression evaluation
cases.assertion_family: decoded record count and fields, expression satisfaction and violation membership, and runtime-scope filtering

feedback.boundary: one upgraded document.artifacts record with nested coordinates/license and runtime scope, plus a simple OR satisfied by one allowed license
feedback.novelty: Feedback has one upgraded record and simple OR only.

final.boundary: three upgraded records with parenthesized AND nested beneath OR, exactly one unsatisfied runtime expression, and development-scope exclusion
final.novelty: The final composes nested AND/OR precedence, parentheses, runtime violation selection, and development-scope filtering across three upgraded records.

cases.prohibited_overlap_finding: Report-schema adaptation and license-expression evaluation contain no request snapshot, category hierarchy, encoding, transport, allocation, reload, checkpoint, or ownership mechanism.

cases.mechanism: inclusive offset-transition lookup plus target-time local-wall-clock to UTC resolution
cases.assertion_family: exact Duration offset equality and exact DateTime next-run equality around a discontinuity

feedback.boundary: the new offset at the exact transition and a later 09:00 wall-clock target resolved with its effective offset
feedback.novelty: Feedback checks the offset boundary and later wall-clock resolution separately.

final.boundary: the newly created 03:00 wall-clock target resolves to the exact UTC transition instant
final.novelty: The final composes the two feedback behaviors at the discontinuity.

cases.prohibited_overlap_finding: The offset is a timezone transition, not a stream/source checkpoint; no other prohibited family is present.

cases.mechanism: Windows segment normalization plus case-insensitive deterministic entry allocation
cases.assertion_family: exact normalized-string equality and sequential allocated-path equality

feedback.boundary: a bare reserved device name, terminal dots/spaces, and a case-only duplicate
feedback.novelty: Feedback checks direct portability and case-only collision behavior.

final.boundary: an extension-bearing reserved basename plus a collision created jointly by normalization and case folding
final.novelty: The final adds an extension-bearing reserved basename and a jointly normalized/case-folded collision.

cases.prohibited_overlap_finding: Filename portability and path allocation are not canonical base64url encoding and do not implement another prohibited family.

cases.mechanism: Profile-selected field/record separators with delimiter-sensitive quoting
cases.assertion_family: Exact rendered-string equality for customer and order rows

feedback.boundary: A non-default pipe field separator and CRLF record separator must be honored for plain customer and order values.
feedback.novelty: Feedback covers profile selection for plain fields.

final.boundary: Values containing the selected semicolon separator must be quoted, and embedded double quotes must be doubled.
final.novelty: The final composes profile selection with delimiter-sensitive quoting and quote escaping.

cases.prohibited_overlap_finding: The profile is read by row-rendering SELECTs; there is no replacement/reload lifecycle, encoding, or canonicalization.

cases.mechanism: Guarded UPDATE state transitions using current state and worker ownership
cases.assertion_family: Affected-row counts plus persisted job state/owner/result equality, including a composed transition sequence

feedback.boundary: A second worker cannot steal an active job, and a non-owner cannot finish a running job.
feedback.novelty: Feedback checks contention and ownership separately.

final.boundary: Canceled and unclaimed queued jobs reject invalid transitions; rejected interference is followed by one owner completion and a rejected terminal replay.
final.novelty: The final adds invalid source states and a multi-step interference/completion/replay composition proving terminal immutability.

cases.prohibited_overlap_finding: Worker ownership is a single job predicate, not composite tenant-stock ownership; the explicit lifecycle is not tri-state inheritance, and no ticket archive/move accounting is present.

cases.mechanism: Lookup-table unit normalization for cubic volume and oversize filtering
cases.assertion_family: Tolerance-based numeric volume equality plus exact ordered row-set equality

feedback.boundary: The 2.54 inch conversion factor applies to all dimensions, and oversize filtering compares converted lengths around 50 centimeters.
feedback.novelty: Feedback uses one factor above one and no exact equality.

final.boundary: Factors below one, all-three-axis conversion, exact threshold equality versus just below, and ordered mixed MM/FT/CM results.
final.novelty: The final adds fractional factors below one, exact inclusive-boundary behavior, multiple units, and composition across all three axes.

cases.prohibited_overlap_finding: Package arithmetic is unit scaling with no allocation, quota, remainder, or fixed-point apportionment.
